title: aquilegia vulgaris subsp. pubiflora
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. pubiflora, also known as the common columbine, is a flowering plant species that is part of the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The roots and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. pubiflora include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
